articles.title: Schools closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, private, govt schools to reopen on this date in Delhi, Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Faridabad, UP, Haryana, Punjab, Chhattisgarh, Mumbai, AP, J-K; Check state-wise reopening dates - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
Schools closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, private, govt schools to reopen on this date in Delhi, Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Faridabad, UP, Haryana, Punjab, Chhattisgarh, Mumbai, AP, J-K; Check state-wise reopening dates
Several states have revised school reopening dates due to the ongoing heatwave. While states such as Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Telangana have resumed classes, Uttar Pradesh has extended summer holidays until 24 June.
Several states across India have revised their school reopening dates as scorching heatwave conditions continue to grip large parts of the country. While some states have already resumed classes after the summer break, others have decided to extend holidays to protect students from extreme temperatures. State governments are closely monitoring the weather and may make further changes if conditions do not improve.
Uttar Pradesh Extends Summer Holidays Until 24 June
The Uttar Pradesh government has extended the summer vacation for schools managed by the Basic Education Council as well as recognised educational institutions until 24 June. The decision was taken after severe heatwave conditions persisted across several districts. Officials said the extension aims to ensure the safety and well-being of students and school staff during the period of extreme heat.
Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh Resume Classes
Schools in Madhya Pradesh reopened on 16 June after the summer holidays, which began on 1 May and continued until 15 June. Students across the state have returned to classrooms as normal. Similarly, schools in Chhattisgarh also resumed regular classes on 16 June, marking the end of the summer vacation and the beginning of the new academic session.
Telangana Delays Reopening Due to Heatwave
The Telangana government postponed the reopening of schools because of intense heat conditions across the state. Educational institutions, which were originally expected to reopen earlier, resumed classes from 15 June. The government said the delay was necessary to safeguard students' health and prevent heat-related illnesses.
Delhi, Haryana and Punjab to Reopen Schools in July
Students in Delhi will return to classrooms on 1 July, giving them one of the longest summer vacations among major states this year. In neighbouring Haryana, summer holidays have been scheduled from 25 May to 30 June, with schools expected to reopen in early July. However, district authorities may alter reopening dates if weather conditions remain severe. Punjab is also expected to reopen schools on 1 July after the completion of the summer break.
Bihar and Rajasthan Resume Schools on 21 June
Schools across Bihar are scheduled to reopen on 21 June following the end of the summer vacation. Rajasthan has also fixed 21 June as the reopening date for educational institutions, allowing students to begin the new academic term after the holiday period.
Jammu and Kashmir to Decide Dates Based on Local Weather
In Jammu and Kashmir, school reopening dates may differ from one region to another. Authorities are expected to issue separate guidelines depending on local weather conditions and temperature levels. Officials will continue reviewing the situation before confirming the final schedule for each area.
Parents Advised to Follow Official School Updates
Education departments have urged parents and students to stay in touch with their schools and regularly check official announcements for any changes. If the heatwave continues, state governments may revise school timings, introduce shorter school hours, or implement additional safety measures to reduce students' exposure to extreme heat.
